title: salix × semiorphana
intro: Salix × semiorphana is a hybrid plant species, specifically a cross between the white willow (Salix alba) and the weeping willow (Salix babylonica), known for its unique characteristics and properties. The bark of Salix × semiorphana contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Salix × semiorphana have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and headaches. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
